tldr: Switch from JVM-focused Develocity to Nx Cloud for JavaScript/TypeScript Nx monorepos with remote caching and distributed task execution.
why_switch: Nx Cloud fits JS/TS monorepos where Develocity targets JVM stacks
Public freemium pricing vs. Develocity's enterprise-only contracts
Tight integration with the Nx CLI, plugins, and Nx Console
Remote computation cache + distributed task execution out of the box
Simpler onboarding for teams already standardized on Nx
what_to_export: Build cache hit/miss telemetry and historical scans (for reference only)
Build scan URLs or archives needed for audits
CI pipeline definitions that wire Develocity into builds
Custom build cache node / replication configuration
export_method: Develocity does not offer a portable cache format; treat historical build scans and cache analytics as reference data only. Use the Develocity API and admin UI to archive any build scans, audit data, and configuration you need to keep before contract end. Refer to Gradle's official Develocity documentation for the current export and retention options.
import_method: Adopt Nx in your repo (if not already) and connect to Nx Cloud via the Nx CLI to enable remote caching and distributed task execution. Update CI pipelines to use Nx commands and to authenticate with Nx Cloud. Replace Develocity-specific build cache plugins and Gradle/Maven hooks. Validate cache correctness on a feature branch before rolling out to main. Consult Nx Cloud documentation for current setup commands and self-hosted options.

concept_mapping.note: Different cache key model; not interchangeable
No 1:1 scan equivalent; UI and depth differ
Different mechanism; based on Nx project graph
Concept similar; configuration and agents differ
Re-create access controls in Nx Cloud

gotchas: Big stack mismatch if your monorepo is primarily JVM (Gradle/Maven/Bazel)
Adopting Nx itself can be a sizable refactor before Nx Cloud helps
Historical build scan data does not migrate; archive what you need
Cache correctness needs validation on a branch before main
Nx Cloud pricing scales with usage; model your CI workload first
Self-hosting Nx Cloud is a premium tier; confirm if needed

email.subject: Migrating monorepo build acceleration from Develocity to Nx Cloud
email.body:
Hi team,

We're evaluating moving from Develocity to Nx Cloud for monorepo build acceleration.

Why:
- Better fit for our JS/TS Nx workflow.
- Public pricing vs. enterprise-only Develocity contracts.
- Tight Nx CLI integration, remote cache, and distributed task execution.

Plan:
1. Archive any Develocity build scans and audit data we need to retain.
2. Wire Nx Cloud into the repo via the Nx CLI and update CI pipelines.
3. Validate cache correctness on a feature branch before rolling to main.
4. Decommission Develocity-specific cache plugins and hooks.

Expected effort: 2-6 weeks depending on how Nx-native our repo already is.

Let me know concerns, especially if any subprojects are JVM-heavy.
